title: About 3 million workers on minimum wage to receive 4.75% pay rise in Fair Work Commission ruling
summary_full: Unions had demanded 6% pay increase for lowest paid after war in Middle East pushed inflation higherFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAbout 100,000 of the country’s lowest paid employees will receive an above-inflation pay rise of 6% from July, as part of the Fair Work Commission’s annual review that also delivered a 4.75% boost to 2.7 million workers on award wages.As the government and unions welcomed the decision, the peak employers’ association warned that “for some small businesses, this will be too much to bear”.
